initial_candidates.sql:
WITH RECURSIVE 
daily_balances AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    txn_date AS balance_date,
    SUM(txn_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY txn_date) AS balance
  FROM customer_transactions
),
date_ranges AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    MIN(txn_date) AS start_date,
    MAX(txn_date) AS end_date
  FROM customer_transactions
  GROUP BY customer_id
),
all_dates AS (
  SELECT start_date AS balance_date, customer_id
  FROM date_ranges
  UNION ALL
  SELECT DATE(balance_date, '+1 day'), customer_id
  FROM all_dates ad
  JOIN date_ranges dr ON ad.customer_id = dr.customer_id
  WHERE balance_date < dr.end_date
),
filled_balances AS (
  SELECT 
    ad.customer_id,
    ad.balance_date,
    COALESCE(db.balance, 
      (SELECT balance 
       FROM daily_balances db2 
       WHERE db2.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
         AND db2.txn_date <= ad.balance_date 
       ORDER BY db2.txn_date DESC 
       LIMIT 1), 0) AS balance
  FROM all_dates ad
  LEFT JOIN daily_balances db ON ad.customer_id = db.customer_id AND ad.balance_date = db.txn_date
),
adjusted_balances AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    balance_date,
    CASE WHEN balance < 0 THEN 0 ELSE balance END AS balance
  FROM filled_balances
),
monthly_max AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date) AS month,
    MAX(balance) AS max_daily_balance
  FROM adjusted_balances
  GROUP BY customer_id, strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date)
)
SELECT 
  month,
  SUM(max_daily_balance) AS total_monthly_balance
FROM monthly_max
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

WITH RECURSIVE 
daily_balances AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    txn_date AS balance_date,
    SUM(txn_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY txn_date) AS daily_balance
  FROM customer_transactions
),
date_ranges AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    MIN(txn_date) AS start_date,
    MAX(txn_date) AS end_date
  FROM customer_transactions
  GROUP BY customer_id
),
all_dates AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    start_date AS balance_date,
    end_date
  FROM date_ranges
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    DATE(balance_date, '+1 day') AS balance_date,
    end_date
  FROM all_dates
  WHERE balance_date < end_date
),
filled_balances AS (
  SELECT 
    ad.customer_id,
    ad.balance_date,
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT db.daily_balance 
       FROM daily_balances db 
       WHERE db.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
         AND db.txn_date <= ad.balance_date 
       ORDER BY db.txn_date DESC 
       LIMIT 1), 
      0
    ) AS daily_balance
  FROM all_dates ad
),
adjusted_balances AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    balance_date,
    CASE 
      WHEN daily_balance < 0 THEN 0 
      ELSE daily_balance 
    END AS daily_balance
  FROM filled_balances
),
monthly_max AS (
  SELECT 
    customer_id,
    strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date) AS month,
    MAX(daily_balance) AS max_daily_balance
  FROM adjusted_balances
  GROUP BY customer_id, strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date)
)
SELECT 
  month,
  SUM(max_daily_balance) AS monthly_total
FROM monthly_max
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

WITH RECURSIVE 
  daily_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      txn_date AS balance_date,
      SUM(txn_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY txn_date) AS running_balance
    FROM customer_transactions
  ),
  date_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      MIN(txn_date) AS start_date,
      MAX(txn_date) AS end_date
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
  ),
  all_dates AS (
    SELECT start_date AS balance_date, customer_id
    FROM date_ranges
    UNION ALL
    SELECT date(balance_date, '+1 day'), customer_id
    FROM all_dates
    WHERE balance_date < (SELECT end_date FROM date_ranges dr WHERE dr.customer_id = all_dates.customer_id)
  ),
  customer_daily_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      ad.customer_id,
      ad.balance_date,
      COALESCE(db.running_balance, 
               (SELECT running_balance 
                FROM daily_balances db2 
                WHERE db2.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
                  AND db2.balance_date <= ad.balance_date 
                ORDER BY db2.balance_date DESC 
                LIMIT 1), 0) AS daily_balance
    FROM all_dates ad
    LEFT JOIN daily_balances db 
      ON ad.customer_id = db.customer_id 
     AND ad.balance_date = db.balance_date
  ),
  adjusted_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      balance_date,
      MAX(daily_balance) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY balance_date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS adjusted_balance
    FROM customer_daily_balances
  ),
  final_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      balance_date,
      CASE WHEN adjusted_balance < 0 THEN 0 ELSE adjusted_balance END AS daily_balance
    FROM adjusted_balances
  ),
  monthly_max_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date) AS month,
      MAX(daily_balance) AS max_daily_balance
    FROM final_balances
    GROUP BY customer_id, strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date)
  )
SELECT 
  month,
  SUM(max_daily_balance) AS monthly_total
FROM monthly_max_balances
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

WITH RECURSIVE daily_balances AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        txn_date AS balance_date,
        SUM(txn_amount) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY txn_date ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) AS running_balance
    FROM customer_transactions
),
date_ranges AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MIN(txn_date) AS start_date,
        MAX(txn_date) AS end_date
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
all_dates AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        start_date AS balance_date
    FROM date_ranges
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
        d.customer_id,
        DATE(d.balance_date, '+1 day') AS balance_date
    FROM all_dates d
    JOIN date_ranges dr ON d.customer_id = dr.customer_id
    WHERE d.balance_date < dr.end_date
),
filled_balances AS (
    SELECT 
        ad.customer_id,
        ad.balance_date,
        COALESCE(
            (SELECT db.running_balance 
             FROM daily_balances db 
             WHERE db.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
               AND db.balance_date <= ad.balance_date 
             ORDER BY db.balance_date DESC 
             LIMIT 1), 
            0
        ) AS daily_balance
    FROM all_dates ad
),
adjusted_balances AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        balance_date,
        CASE WHEN daily_balance < 0 THEN 0 ELSE daily_balance END AS daily_balance
    FROM filled_balances
),
monthly_max_balances AS (
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date) AS month,
        MAX(daily_balance) AS max_daily_balance
    FROM adjusted_balances
    GROUP BY customer_id, strftime('%Y-%m', balance_date)
)
SELECT 
    month,
    SUM(max_daily_balance) AS monthly_total_max_balance
FROM monthly_max_balances
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

WITH RECURSIVE
  customer_dates AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      MIN(txn_date) AS min_date,
      MAX(txn_date) AS max_date
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
  ),
  all_days AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      min_date AS txn_date
    FROM customer_dates
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
      ad.customer_id,
      DATE(ad.txn_date, '+1 day') AS txn_date
    FROM all_days ad
    JOIN customer_dates cd ON ad.customer_id = cd.customer_id
    WHERE ad.txn_date < cd.max_date
  ),
  daily_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      ad.customer_id,
      ad.txn_date,
      COALESCE(
        (SELECT SUM(ct.txn_amount) 
         FROM customer_transactions ct 
         WHERE ct.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
           AND ct.txn_date <= ad.txn_date),
        0
      ) - COALESCE(
        (SELECT SUM(ct.txn_amount) 
         FROM customer_transactions ct 
         WHERE ct.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
           AND ct.txn_date < ad.txn_date 
           AND ct.txn_type = 'refund'), -- Assuming negative amounts may represent refunds, but per schema only txn_amount is numeric; no explicit refund type. So we assume txn_amount can be negative.
        0
      ) AS raw_balance
    FROM all_days ad
  ),
  daily_balances_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      txn_date,
      MAX(0, raw_balance) AS daily_balance
    FROM daily_balances
  ),
  monthly_max_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      strftime('%Y-%m', txn_date) AS month,
      MAX(daily_balance) AS max_daily_balance
    FROM daily_balances_cleaned
    GROUP BY customer_id, strftime('%Y-%m', txn_date)
  )
SELECT 
  month,
  SUM(max_daily_balance) AS monthly_total_max_balance
FROM monthly_max_balances
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;

aggregation.selected_sql:
WITH RECURSIVE
  customer_dates AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      MIN(txn_date) AS min_date,
      MAX(txn_date) AS max_date
    FROM customer_transactions
    GROUP BY customer_id
  ),
  all_days AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      min_date AS txn_date
    FROM customer_dates
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 
      ad.customer_id,
      DATE(ad.txn_date, '+1 day') AS txn_date
    FROM all_days ad
    JOIN customer_dates cd ON ad.customer_id = cd.customer_id
    WHERE ad.txn_date < cd.max_date
  ),
  daily_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      ad.customer_id,
      ad.txn_date,
      COALESCE(
        (SELECT SUM(ct.txn_amount) 
         FROM customer_transactions ct 
         WHERE ct.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
           AND ct.txn_date <= ad.txn_date),
        0
      ) - COALESCE(
        (SELECT SUM(ct.txn_amount) 
         FROM customer_transactions ct 
         WHERE ct.customer_id = ad.customer_id 
           AND ct.txn_date < ad.txn_date 
           AND ct.txn_type = 'refund'), -- Assuming negative amounts may represent refunds, but per schema only txn_amount is numeric; no explicit refund type. So we assume txn_amount can be negative.
        0
      ) AS raw_balance
    FROM all_days ad
  ),
  daily_balances_cleaned AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      txn_date,
      MAX(0, raw_balance) AS daily_balance
    FROM daily_balances
  ),
  monthly_max_balances AS (
    SELECT 
      customer_id,
      strftime('%Y-%m', txn_date) AS month,
      MAX(daily_balance) AS max_daily_balance
    FROM daily_balances_cleaned
    GROUP BY customer_id, strftime('%Y-%m', txn_date)
  )
SELECT 
  month,
  SUM(max_daily_balance) AS monthly_total_max_balance
FROM monthly_max_balances
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;
